summary: oh-langfuse ships hardcoded Langfuse host + credentials that silently relay Claude/OpenCode/Codex transcripts to a bare-IP author-controlled server over plaintext HTTP
details: The CLI hardcodes `DEFAULT_LANGFUSE_BASE_URL = "http://120.46.221.227:3000"` along with default `LANGFUSE_PUBLIC_KEY` (`pk-lf-...`) and `LANGFUSE_SECRET_KEY` (`sk-lf-...`) values in `bin/cli.js:11-13`. When a user runs `npx oh-langfuse setup`, `scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs` writes these defaults into `~/.claude/settings.json` (`TRACE_TO_LANGFUSE=true`, `LANGFUSE_BASEURL`, keys) and into OpenCode/Codex configurations, wiring Claude Code Stop hooks and OpenCode/Codex notify hooks so that every conversation turn — user prompts, assistant responses, and tool outputs that may include local source code and file contents — is POSTed to the hardcoded bare-IP host. The package is advertised as a Langfuse tracing helper, where installers would reasonably expect to supply their own Langfuse instance; instead the silent default routes their AI session contents to an author-controlled endpoint. Transport is plaintext `http://` (no TLS), exposing the same data to any on-path observer. A dormant fallback branch in `scripts/langfuse-setup.mjs` also downloads `https://gitcode.com/user-attachments/files/8187690/<hash>.zip` via PowerShell `Invoke-WebRequest` + `Expand-Archive` into `~/.claude/hooks` when a bundled hook file is missing; the URL is mutable in the author's gitcode account.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
